title: End of module: Research Frontiers in CSP

questions.text: Which source is recommended as the first thing to read, before Gardner's dense 1987 foundational text?
choices: Gardner, Napolitano & Paura's 2006 survey paper, "Cyclostationarity: Half a Century of Research"
questions.explanation: The 2006 survey paper orients a reader across the whole field's history and structure at a gentler pace before committing to Gardner's denser 1987 book, which is the standard recommended reading order.

questions.text: Which paper is the reference for the FAM and SSCA cyclic-spectrum-estimation algorithms?
choices: Roberts, Brown & Loomis, "Computationally Efficient Algorithms for Cyclic Spectral Analysis" (1991)
Gardner's "Statistical Spectral Analysis: A Nonprobabilistic Theory" (1987)
questions.explanation: Roberts, Brown & Loomis (1991, IEEE Signal Processing Magazine) is the standard reference for the FAM and SSCA algorithms covered in the cyclic-spectrum-estimation module.

questions.text: Why is efficient real-time hardware implementation of cyclic spectral estimators considered a legitimately open research area, despite the underlying theory being decades old?
choices: A field's theory being mature doesn't make the engineering of computing it efficiently on real hardware, in real time, equally settled
The theory itself is still actively disputed and unsettled
No practical application actually needs real-time cyclic spectral estimation
questions.explanation: Theory maturity and engineering-implementation maturity are separate axes — CSP's core theory dates to the 1980s, but efficiently mapping FAM/SSCA-style algorithms onto real hardware for real-time use remains an active area, not a finished exercise.

questions.text: The recommended way to start engaging with CSP research, given an existing SDR/GNU Radio background, is to:
choices: Reproduce 2-3 published results computationally first, using the existing pipeline as a testbed, before attempting to extend them
Attempt a fully novel cycle-frequency estimator immediately
Avoid writing any code until every recommended text has been read twice
questions.explanation: Reproducing established results first validates your tooling and understanding before attempting anything original — the same working-code checkpoint-project philosophy used throughout this course.

questions.text: Who writes an active CSP blog described as a useful bridge between textbook derivations and applied, worked examples?
questions.explanation: Chad Spooner's CSP blog is specifically called out as a good bridge between the denser textbook material (Gardner, Napolitano) and applied, worked examples once the fundamentals have landed.

questions.text: In what year was William A. Gardner's foundational text "Statistical Spectral Analysis: A Nonprobabilistic Theory" published?
questions.explanation: Gardner's foundational text was published in 1987, and remains the source most later CSP work (including the survey paper and Napolitano's 2020 follow-up) builds on and cites.

questions.text: One surname: the author of the 2020 book "Cyclostationarity: New Trends and Applications", the modern follow-up filling gaps in Gardner's older book, is ___.
questions.explanation: Antonio Napolitano's 2020 book is the modern follow-up to Gardner's 1987 foundational text, filling gaps the older book leaves.

questions.text: One word: efficient real-time implementation of cyclic spectral estimators on dedicated hardware (e.g. FPGAs) is flagged as a legitimately under-served research ___.
questions.explanation: The module explicitly frames hardware-efficient real-time CSP estimation as an under-served research area — a natural fit for a background in FPGA/embedded signal processing.

questions.text: Cumulative review: across this whole course's case, the single reason every purely PSD-based tool missed the recurring signal's structure was:
choices: The ordinary PSD is only the α = 0 slice of the full spectral correlation function, and the signal's structure lived at nonzero cycle frequencies
The recording was simply too short to reveal any spectral structure at all
PSD-based tools cannot be applied to real recorded data, only to simulations
questions.explanation: From cyclostationarity-core-theory: the ordinary PSD is structurally restricted to α = 0. The signal's clocked, modulated structure lived at nonzero cycle frequencies tied to its symbol rate — a dimension no amount of careful PSD estimation could ever reveal, which is exactly why the course's case needed the cyclic-spectrum tools built up across the later modules.
